page: Amazon Web Services c8id.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The processor is equipped with a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for matrix operations. The instance is configured with 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, yielding a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 2.0 GB per core.

This instance includes 950 GB of bundled local NVMe SSD storage, providing high-speed direct-attached storage. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. The instance does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Benchmark data shows strong single-threaded performance, with Geekbench single-core scores of 2219.0 and Geekbench text processing scores of 2967.0 reaching the top 10% tier. Single-threaded gzip compression at 34.49 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.44 GB/sec perform in the top 25% tier. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 13435.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 17299.69, both falling in the middle 50% average. Memory bandwidth benchmarks indicate 1284.52 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 136.59 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. For machine learning workloads, LLM inference text generation using a small 135M model achieves a top-tier speed of 390.86 tokens/sec, while prompt processing for the same model averages 1733.8 tokens/sec.

Given its dedicated compute resources, DDR5 memory, and integrated NVMe SSD storage, the c8id.4xlarge offers a balanced resource density for compute-bound workloads requiring fast local scratch space. The inclusion of AMX and AVX-512 instructions provides hardware-level acceleration for vector and matrix operations without requiring a dedicated GPU. This instance is suited for workloads such as software compilation, text processing, database operations, and small-to-medium scale language model inference.
description: Amazon Web Services c8id.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized instance powered by an 8-core Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz with 16 dedicated vCPUs. It features 32.0 GB of DDR5 memory and a 480 MB L3 cache, alongside 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it provides 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results highlight top-tier single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core and text processing scores in the top 10%, and single-threaded gzip compression in the top 25%. Multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally align with the average middle tier. The processor supports AVX-512 and Intel AMX instructions, enhancing vector and matrix calculations. This instance is suited for software compilation, text processing, database operations, and small-to-medium scale language model inference.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c8id.4xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 16 vCPUs, 32 GB DDR5 RAM, and 950 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for text processing and compilation.

tagline: An x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and integrated local NVMe SSD storage for high-speed data access.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor at 3.9 GHz.
32.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory and 950 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and text processing benchmarks.
Hardware-accelerated vector and matrix operations via Intel AMX and AVX-512.
8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth managed by the Nitro hypervisor.
Suited for software compilation, database operations, and small-scale LLM inference.
